title: CyberBoard Config (official / IR format)
description: The config JSON written by AM Master and produced by the merger — keymap + LED display in one document. This is the IR (intermediate representation) the CLI builds toward and writes. Field confidence is noted per-key via $comment (see .claude/rules/10-config-schema.md, 30-write-protocol.md). 🟢 confirmed / 🟡 inferred / 🔴 unverified.
$comment: additionalProperties is true at the root: community/base configs flatten-preserve fields we don't model yet (merger keeps non-LED props verbatim). Core fields are validated; unknown extras are allowed.

product_info.description: Product identity block. Sample files ship placeholder 'CB_XX'; the real R4 reports 'CB04' via the [1,1] product_id query — not from this field.

page_num.description: Number of LED display pages. R4 ships 8.

page_data.description: LED display pages. index 0-4 = built-in (battery/mosaic/time/text/streamer), 5/6/7 = Custom LED 1/2/3 = the user-facing 'slots'.

Fn_key.description: Fn-hold key substitutions. Note: not present in the R-series send order (§5) — exact wire behaviour unconfirmed 🔴.

MACRO_key.description: Macros (key sequence + per-step interval). Uppercase MACRO_key; distinct from the lowercase (empty) macro_key.

swap_key.description: Key swaps (input -> out). Sent via [6,6], 11 per frame. 🟢

exchange_key.description: Key exchanges (array in/out). Sent via [6,1], one frame per entry — note the wire sends every list entry regardless of exchange_num. 🟢

macro_key.description: Lowercase, usually empty. Distinct from MACRO_key.

spotlight_frames.description: Car-light / spotlight (CMD_CAR_*). Presence on R4 unconfirmed 🔴 — absent in known merger outputs.

hatsu.description: HATSU-product page/line data. Not present on R4. 🔴

boolOrInt.$comment: 'valid' fields appear as bool, int (0/1/2), OR numeric string across sources; change_dict / serde normalise these (0->False, non-0->True). The 1-vs-2 distinction is unconfirmed 🟡.

numberish.$comment: Integer that some sources serialise as a quoted string (e.g. frame_index '0' on inactive-page placeholders). serde accepts both.

comment.$comment: '//' keys carry human comments (often Chinese) inside otherwise-strict objects; allow them everywhere.

hexColor.description: #RRGGBB (3 bytes via rgb_to_bytes). Strict — used for color blocks.

frameColor.description: Per-pixel frame color. Active pages use #RRGGBB; inactive-page placeholders carry a short malformed value like '#0000' (length-1 frame_RGB), so the pattern is loosened here.

keyCode.description: #MMPPUUUU — MM=modifier bitmask, PP=HID usage page (07=keyboard, 0C=consumer, 92=AM vendor Fn), UUUU=usage id. 4 bytes. #00000000 = unassigned.

unicodePoint.description: #XXXX — a 2-byte code point for text pages (unicode_to_bytes).

rgbFrameSet.description: A set of RGB frames. For display 'frames' each frame_RGB has 200 entries (40x5); for per-key 'keyframes' it has 90 (one per key). 🟢 Active pages (5/6/7) are full-size; inactive pages carry a length-1 placeholder with frame_num 0.

frame_num.$comment: REAL per-slot limit = 256 (2^8), confirmed on hardware 2026-06-22: writing 400 numbered frames ACKs but plays back only 0-255 (firmware's playback index is 8-bit). merger's MAX_FRAMES=300 and AM Master's ~300 both overshoot the true 256; the official 300-frame editor produces 257-300 'dead' frames that never display. The wire field is 2 bytes so the PROTOCOL would allow up to 32767, but the schema enforces the firmware ceiling so cb_verify flags un-playable frame counts before a write. per-slot-vs-total still unconfirmed 🔴.

frame_data.$comment: Capped at the firmware playback ceiling (256). See frame_num above.

frame_RGB.description: Per-pixel colors. Note the JSON key is upper-case frame_RGB (serde-renamed to frame_rgb internally).

frame_num.$comment: 256 by analogy with the confirmed 8-bit playback cursor on display/per-key frames; spotlight itself is unverified on R4 🔴.

keyframes.description: 90 per-key backlight frames (one color per key). NOT interpolation keyframes. This is the 'lighting' subsystem in AM Master, whose UI caps a single animation at 100 frames 🟡 (vs the display editor's 300). That 100 is only a UI authoring cap, NOT a firmware limit: the known-good merger output merged_20250916_161615.json has 123 keyframes on page 7 (via merger 'combine') and writes/ACKs fine. The per-key frame_index is sent as 1 byte ([5,pi] [2]=frame_index) so the structural ceiling is 256; the real firmware per-key PLAYBACK limit is untested 🔴 (could be ≤100 like display's 256<300, but per-key can't be numbered-rendered to count). Schema enforces the structural 256 (via rgbFrameSet), not the 100 UI cap, so it doesn't reject the 123-frame known-good.

keyLayer.description: Per-layer remap matrix. Sent via [6,8] (layer count) then [6,7] (60-byte chunks). 🟢

layer.description: Reserved 25x8 = 200 matrix positions of HID keycodes.

inOutKey.description: A single input->out key mapping (Fn_key / swap_key share this shape).
